skill.name: rag-implementation
skill.description: Build Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems with document loading, chunking, embeddings, vector stores, retrieval patterns, reranking, prompts, and evaluation.
skill.summary: Implement grounded RAG applications with practical retrieval, vector database, and evaluation guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The repeated external command findings are false positives from markdown code fences, not executable shell or Ruby backticks. The skill is documentation-only, but its examples describe reading local documents and connecting to embedding or vector database services, so publication risk is low with normal user caution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick findings are false positives. The cited locations are fenced Python examples in SKILL.md, not executable Ruby, shell, or skill runtime code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown fence delimiters or adjacent documentation examples. No executable skill script or Ruby shell backtick pattern is present.

low_findings.title: Example Integrations Use Local Files and Service Clients
low_findings.description: The skill includes sample code for loading ./docs, persisting a Chroma database, and configuring OpenAI, Pinecone, and Weaviate clients. These are normal RAG examples, but users should protect source data and credentials when adapting them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly reference local files and service clients, but the file is instructional markdown. I found no evidence of automatic execution or credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Critical Combination Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combined markdown code fences, example service URLs, and placeholder API key text. Review found no prompt injection attempt, obfuscation, malicious endpoint, real secret, or automatic network transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed span is ordinary RAG documentation with Python snippets. Suspicious intent was not supported by the surrounding context.

content.user_title: Build RAG Systems for LLM Apps
content.value_statement: Teams need LLM answers that stay grounded in trusted documents. This skill guides retrieval, chunking, vector storage, reranking, prompting, and evaluation for practical RAG systems.
actual_capabilities: Explains core RAG components, including vector databases, embeddings, retrieval, and reranking.
Provides LangChain-style examples for document loading, chunking, vector stores, and retrieval chains.
Compares retrieval patterns such as hybrid search, multi-query retrieval, contextual compression, and parent document retrieval.
Shows prompt patterns for grounded answers, citations, and confidence scoring.
Outlines evaluation metrics for answer accuracy, retrieval quality, and groundedness.
limitations: It is a guidance skill, not a complete production RAG application.
Some library imports may require updates for current LangChain versions.
It does not include deployment, authentication, or tenant isolation designs.
Users must choose secure storage and access controls for sensitive documents.

use_cases.title: Build Internal Document Q&A
use_cases.description: Create a retrieval pipeline that answers employee questions from approved internal documents.

use_cases.title: Improve Search Relevance
use_cases.description: Combine semantic search, keyword search, metadata filters, and reranking for better retrieval quality.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Grounded Answers
use_cases.description: Define test cases and metrics that measure answer accuracy, retrieval quality, and grounding.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic RAG App
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a small document corpus.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-implementation skill to plan a basic RAG app for my document collection. Include ingestion, chunking, embeddings, vector storage, retrieval, and answer generation.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Retrieval Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Select retrieval methods for an existing product.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-implementation skill to compare dense, sparse, hybrid, multi-query, and reranking options for my use case. Recommend a retrieval design with tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Design Evaluation Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-implementation skill to design an evaluation plan for a RAG system. Include test cases, retrieval metrics, groundedness checks, and failure analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-implementation skill to review my proposed RAG architecture for scaling, latency, chunking, metadata filtering, reranking, citations, and security boundaries.

output_examples.input: Plan a RAG assistant for product manuals.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation plan covering document ingestion, chunk sizes, embedding selection, vector database setup, retriever configuration, citation prompts, and evaluation steps.

output_examples.input: Improve weak retrieval results for support articles.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist that recommends metadata filters, hybrid search, query rewriting, reranking, and measurement of relevant document recall.

output_examples.input: Evaluate if answers are grounded in sources.
output_examples.output: A test approach with expected answers, relevant source documents, retrieval quality scoring, answer accuracy checks, and groundedness review.

best_practices: Start with a clear corpus, access rules, update cadence, and measurable answer quality targets.
Tune chunk size, overlap, metadata, retrieval count, and reranking with real user queries.
Return citations and monitor retrieval quality so teams can inspect answer grounding.
anti_patterns: Do not index sensitive documents without access controls and redaction rules.
Do not rely only on vector similarity when keyword precision or metadata filters are required.
Do not ship a RAG system without evaluation cases for common and failure-prone questions.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design and implement RAG systems that answer questions using retrieved source documents.

faq.question: Does it include a complete application?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples that users adapt to their own stack.

faq.question: Which vector databases are covered?
faq.answer: It discusses Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, Chroma, Qdrant, and FAISS at a high level.

faq.question: Can it help reduce hallucinations?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes grounded prompts, retrieved context, citations, and evaluation.

faq.question: Does it manage secrets for me?
faq.answer: No. Users must store API keys securely and avoid hardcoding real credentials.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
